aquacroptools 0.1.0
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Regional Crop Simulations Using aquacroptools

Read an AquaCrop CLI File

Source: R/readers.R
read_cli.Rd

Reads and parses an AquaCrop CLI (climate) file, locates the referenced weather files (.Tnx, .ETo, .PLU), reads them, and joins them into a single tidy data frame.

read_cli(file)

read_climate(file)

Arguments

file

Character. Path to the AquaCrop CLI file.

Value

A tibble with columns:

  • station: Character, station/grid name (from temperature file)

  • year: Integer, year

  • month: Integer, month (1-12)

  • day: Integer, day of month

  • tmin: Numeric, minimum temperature (°C)

  • tmax: Numeric, maximum temperature (°C)

  • eto: Numeric, reference evapotranspiration (mm/day)

  • rain: Numeric, rainfall (mm)

Details

The CLI file structure:

  • Line 1: Description/name

  • Line 2: AquaCrop version

  • Line 3: Temperature file (.Tnx)

  • Line 4: ETo file (.ETo)

  • Line 5: Rainfall file (.PLU)

  • Line 6: CO2 file (.CO2)

File path resolution:

  • If filename only (e.g., "grid_005.Tnx"), looks in the same directory as the CLI file

  • If absolute path (e.g., "/data/weather/grid_005.Tnx"), uses that path

  • If relative path (e.g., "../weather/grid_005.Tnx"), resolves relative to CLI file location

The function reads all three weather files and joins them by date columns (station, year, month, day) using left joins.

See also

Other AquaCrop readers: read_eto(), read_plu(), read_season_out(), read_tnx()

Other AquaCrop readers: read_eto(), read_plu(), read_season_out(), read_tnx()

Examples

if (FALSE) { # \dontrun{
# Read CLI file and get complete weather data
climate <- read_cli("data/default.CLI")
head(climate)

# Check for missing data
summary(climate)

# Filter complete cases
complete_data <- climate[complete.cases(climate), ]
} # }
